texto: He had not been sleeping well over Christmas. Actually, he hadn’t been doing anything well over Christmas – eating, sleeping, exercising, talking, looking after himself, laughing, crying… No, he hadn’t really been crying despite all the pain he felt. It was just tearing him up inside, quietly. It was like his insides were being ripped up by an angered tiger.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Pamela Harju: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a humor. O trecho “He had not been sleeping well over Christmas. Actually, he hadn’t been doing anything well over C…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, coming-of-age, coming-of-age-novel pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e natal.

frase_original: He had not been sleeping well over Christmas. Actually, he hadn’t been doing anything well over Christmas – eating, sleeping, exercising, talking, looking after himself, laughing, crying… No, he hadn’t really been crying despite all the pain he felt. It was just tearing him up inside, quietly. It was like his insides were being ripped up by an angered tiger.

texto: Mel eventually gave up trying to talk to him and just sat there looking at him. There was not likely to be much to look at, apart from the red, puffy eyes, runny nose and the rest of the mess that was Kyle French. Certainly not the once good-looking young student that Mel had developed a crush on.
